steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize similarity between the two materials. Both undergo torsional heating when twisted, even though their temperature increases differ (3.5°C vs 21.3°C). The correct answer treats them as sharing the same behavior.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘In 2019, two research teams observed the effects of torsional heating on various fibers, including SEBS rubber and single-ply NiTi wire.’: Mentions both materials but does not establish that they share the same behavior. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Both SEBS rubber and single-ply NiTi wire fibers heat up when twisted, according to a 2019 study.’: 'Both' directly signals similarity, and a single shared behavior is stated. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘Researchers determined that when the fibers were twisted, the average surface temperature of single-ply NiTi wire increased more than that of SEBS rubber.’: Explicitly contrasts their different temperature changes — emphasizes difference, not similarity. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Twisting an object will generally cause its temperature to increase, a process known as torsional heating.’: Generalizes about torsional heating without focusing on the two specific materials. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
